# HPD.ML.Abstractions.IDataHandle

- Package: `HPD-ML-Abstractions` `0.5.0`
- Assembly: `HPD.ML.Abstractions`
- Namespace: `HPD.ML.Abstractions`
- Kind: `interface`
- Guide: [Related documentation](../../../concepts/data-handles-rows-and-cursors.md)

A format-agnostic, lazy reference to schema-bearing data. Consumers choose their materialization strategy.

## Declaration

```csharp
public interface IDataHandle
```

## Properties

### Capabilities

```csharp
public HPD.ML.Abstractions.MaterializationCapabilities Capabilities { get; }
```

Reports which fast-path materialization modes are available.

### Ordering

```csharp
public HPD.ML.Abstractions.OrderingPolicy Ordering { get; }
```

Ordering guarantee provided by this handle.

### RowCount

```csharp
public System.Nullable<long> RowCount { get; }
```

Total row count, or null if unknown (streaming/lazy source).

### Schema

```csharp
public HPD.ML.Abstractions.ISchema Schema { get; }
```

The schema describing columns, types, and annotations.

## Methods

### GetCursor

```csharp
public abstract HPD.ML.Abstractions.IRowCursor GetCursor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<string> columnsNeeded)
```

Forward-only, row-by-row cursor. Universal baseline.

### Materialize

```csharp
public abstract HPD.ML.Abstractions.IDataHandle Materialize()
```

Fully-materialized, in-memory copy with columnar access.

### StreamRows

```csharp
public abstract System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable<HPD.ML.Abstractions.IRow> StreamRows(System.Threading.CancellationToken ct = null)
```

Async row stream with ordering and cancellation.

### TryGetColumnBatch

```csharp
public abstract bool TryGetColumnBatch<T>(string columnName, int startRow, int rowCount, out System.Numerics.Tensors.ReadOnlyTensorSpan<T> batch)
```

Zero-copy tensor view of a typed column batch. Returns false if unavailable (caller falls back to cursor). For scalar columns: shape [rowCount]. For vector columns: shape [rowCount, ...dims].
